response: Bat, the amateur historian can determine the number of average purchases by first calculating the total value of the tokens issued. Each token was worth 3 pence, and the village issued 240 tokens, so the total value is 240 tokens × 3 pence/token = 720 pence. If each average purchase cost 18 pence, then the number of purchases that could be made is 720 pence ÷ 18 pence/purchase = 40 purchases. Therefore, the total number of average purchases that could be made with the tokens issued in that year is 40. P.P.S.: lake.
